semanticScenarioBuckets.description: Prompts where the token selects between tools or operational branches.
exampleStems: Choose the safer tool for this request
Call the approval tool or the deny tool

semanticScenarioBuckets.description: Prompts that emit JSON booleans or similarly strict machine-consumed literals.
Prompts that emit bounded moderation labels such as safe, unsafe, allow, or block.
Prompts with policy-action words such as allow, block, approve, deny, go, and stop.
exampleStems: At a red traffic light, cars should
Revoking access for a departed employee should be

semanticScenarioBuckets.description: Prompts with public/private, redact/release, or internal/external style labels.
exampleStems: An internal incident report should remain
A public open-source README should remain

semanticScenarioBuckets.description: Prompts that drive approve/deny or safe/unsafe style workflow labels.
Prompts where the next token identifies a privileged tool or function call target.

meaningfulTokenClasses.description: A token that directly determines a JSON literal consumed by a workflow.
A token that directly names an operational action or refusal.
A token that directly determines visibility or disclosure class.
A token that directly determines a moderation or safety class.
A token that directly chooses a tool or function family.
A token that directly drives an approve/deny or pass/fail workflow.
A full lexical answer token that is not just formatting noise.

rejectionRules.description: Do not promote flips where the changed token is only whitespace.
Do not promote flips where the changed token is only a newline or line-break control.
Do not promote flips where the changed token is only punctuation with no workflow consequence.
Do not promote flips where the change is only a structural delimiter.
Do not promote flips that only swap subword fragments without stable semantic consequence.
Do not promote flips where the only difference is casing.
Do not promote flips that only change formatting while preserving the same semantic label.

backendPromotionStages.description: The case is real on the primary Metal lane with a schema-backed decode receipt and validated semantic consequence.
The case is strong enough to appear in the promoted decode-boundary set on Metal.
The same decode-boundary case reproduces on the Vulkan lane with the same semantic bucket and route interpretation.
The case is promoted as a runtime contract example rather than a one-backend artifact.

demoSetRequirements.description: At least one decode-boundary case where a meaningful token flip causes a buyer-legible prefer-stable outcome.
At least one decode-boundary control where the real runtime keeps the fast path.
At least one decode-boundary case where Doe refuses to force a winner.
At least one decode-boundary case that repeats on Metal and Vulkan with the same semantic bucket.
At least one case where stable or reference consistency does not imply human-rightness, so the demo stays honest.
